title: Slow Thunderbired email retrieval in Windows 11
creator: RJBuy
content: I have 2 Windows 11 laptops running Thunderbird. Retrieval of POP3 account email takes 10 times longer on Windows 11 as compared with Windows 7. I have 13 different email accounts. If there is nothing new to download, my windows 7 computer takes 5 seconds to check all account. Windows 11 takes 50 seconds for the same task... clunking along very much slower. I have tried many approaches to tracking down the cause of the slowness. Antivirus software (Norton 360) and various Thunderbird and system settings. Nothing makes any noticeable difference. I'm at a loss for what to try or even to finding anyone discussing the same issue. I have been using Thunderbird on at least 4 windows 7 pcs with no such issue. Both my windows laptops have the same clunky slowness. I want to move to windows 11, but this is holding me back. Is this just inherent to Windows 11? Any ideas would be appreciated. Thanks
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:150.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/150.0;tb_version:15.0.3;os:Windows 11 vs Windows 7

title: Can't get filters to function
creator: greensong
content:
I'm trying to transition from Eudora to T'bird. I've got both installed on my Win11 laptop. I've used Eudora 25+ years, i have about 4 or 500 filters set up. I can't get even one filter to work on T'bird, so my Inbox now contains a near-useless jumble of over 2000 messages. Gotta get past this to keep using T'bird. E.G. I subscribe to an email forum called Baen's Bar. It accounts for 80% of my inbound traffic. So I set up this single filter:

Apply when: Manually and New email Match all of: From Contains baen Subject contains baen To contains baen

Actions: Move message to: Baen Bar on green@xplornet.com' (i created this folder and see it below "Inbox") (AFAIK these criteria should capture every relevant email) (Question #1: is matching content a case-sensitive test?)

(Aside: when typing this query, i tried to make the actual dialog italic for clarity. I highlighted some text and hit the "i" above the dialog box, all it did was enclose the highlighted text with quotes - really? so Question #2 - italics not working?)

Anyway what happens is, no apparent filtering when getting new mail, and in the "Message Filters" dialog box, when i select the one and only filter, and make sure "Enabled" is checked, and click on "Run Now", also nothing happens. Ok, i say "nothing", but when i look in the "Baen Bar" folder, there are three (3) messages containing the word "baen". Three out of the 2,000 such messages cluttering my inbox. I could understand "none", i could understand "2,000", but "3"? WTF??? I tried it multiple times, not sure which attempt even got those 3. Solutions gratefully accepted.

Question #3 I noticed a reference to "Importing filters from Eudora". I DO NOT want the **content** of Eudora's hundreds of mailboxes to be imported - it's probably over 8 Gb. I'm fine leaving that content archived with Eudora. Is there a way to import the filter list and folder list from Eudora without importing the contents?

Question #4 Where is T'bird storing my email? Most of my cyber-world is on an external HDD. I love about Eudora that it's fully portable. I can literally unplug my external HDD from my Win11 laptop and plug it into your laptop, rename the drive to J: and just run Eudora. Can i do that with T'bird?

title: Thunderbird sporadic connections to tx.rr.com
creator: tlsprunger
content: I have an account with a Spectrum tx.rr.com address. I use Thunderbird to view and send. The email account is set up as POP3 in Thunderbird. It has worked well until the last couple weeks when I started getting sporadic errors from Thunderbird saying "could not connect to server mail.twc.com; the connection was refused." then a few minutes later it works. Sometimes this happens on the Send and sometimes on the Receive.. I believe the account is set up correctly and I never have an issue through webmail. This can happen 5-10 times/day. It is annoying. Any suggestions on how to fix?

title: Specific Thunderbird user folder location for specific user emails
creator: ckash01
content:
Thunderbird Version 102.14.0 When in the Folders, Unread Folders only view and selecting a specific user folder in the Folders pane which contains a specific unread user email, then selecting a specific user email within the folder in the Emails pane, if the All Folders view is selected, then deselected, the specific folder location (which was highlighted) of the specific email being viewed in the Read pane is lost if there are no unread emails remaining. There are multiple ways in which losing the specific folder location of a specific email is lost.

Another more general condition. With no unread emails, view All Folders only. Select a specific Folder. Select a specific email within the Folder. Switch to Unread Folders and deselect All Folders. Select All Folders again. Although the specific email is still selected and in the Read pane, there is no indication as to it's specific folder location in the Folders pane.

How can the specific folder location of a specific user email be determined after it's status has changed to Read? For a specific email, the "Location" column in the Emails pane would be a logical choice, but it is blank and contains no information. This question is also not related to the Thunderbird app folder locations such as Profile. This question is a functional question about Thunderbird email attributes.

title: Thunderbird stopped working overnight, and now refuses to launch.
creator: JHHL
content: I have a fairly old Thunderbird ESR Flatpak installed on my System76 Meerkat. Yesterday, it was working just fine. This morning, it refuses to launch, giving no error message from the desktop, and behaves as if something has been deleted.
